topic: New flagship topic: exact non-equivalence frontier among three named invariance-based causal estimators in linear Gaussian SEMs with anchors and environments: Invariant Causal Prediction by Peters, Buhlmann, and Meinshausen 2016, Anchor Regression by Rothenhausler, Meinshausen, Buhlmann, and Peters 2021 JRSSB, and Causal Dantzig by Rothenhausler, Buhlmann, and Meinshausen 2019. Pre-anchor check: closest published theorem is Anchor Regression Section 2, which shows the population anchor path interpolates between partialling-out, OLS, and IV and gives robustness under shift perturbations; closest adjacent anchors are ICP invariant conditional-distribution parent-set theorem and Causal Dantzig linear residual-invariance equations under additive interventions. Our theorem is not that because the focal object is a finite covariance-level conflict certificate comparing the three independently defined procedures on the same SEM: derive from their published equations an explicit anchor-shift covariance pencil K(Sigma_XYA), characterize the sharp rank and kernel frontier where ICP accepted sets, anchor-regression gamma-infinity coefficients, and causal-Dantzig coefficients coincide, and exhibit a 2-anchor by 2-covariate Gaussian SEM witness where pairwise residual moment invariance holds but conditional-distribution invariance fails and the converse. If the proposal reduces to generic projection algebra, k-class or IV restatement, ordinary ICP sufficiency, or defining K as the disagreement itself, pivot or stop early. Require a concrete nonroutine object: an entrywise hand-derived K matrix from named estimating equations, a non-equivalence witness class with all covariances shown, and an iff frontier not equivalent to LP duality, support coverage, path derivatives, or definition-unfolding.

open_problems.open_problem: Derive a primitive covariance pencil K(Sigma_XYA) whose zero set is exactly the simultaneous and pairwise agreement frontier among ICP accepted sets, the gamma-infinity anchor-regression coefficient, and Causal-Dantzig coefficients.

source_refs.claim: ICP defines causal predictors by equality of conditional distributions Y|X_S across environments, not by a shared linear residual moment equation.
source_refs.author: Peters, Buhlmann, Meinshausen

source_refs.claim: Anchor regression gives a population path interpolating between partialling-out, OLS, and IV/TSLS and robustness to anchor-span shifts.
source_refs.author: Rothenhausler, Meinshausen, Buhlmann, Peters

source_refs.claim: Causal Dantzig uses residual inner-product invariance under additive interventions in linear SEMs with hidden variables.
source_refs.author: Rothenhausler, Buhlmann, Meinshausen

source_refs.excerpt: replacing span equality with an explicit null pairwise projection-gap condition
Conjecture 1: spectral uniform non-equivalence frontier (NOT YET PROVED)

open_problems.why_unsolved: The published papers define three different invariance objects: ICP uses equality of conditional laws, anchor regression uses anchor-penalized population normal equations with IV-like limits, and Causal Dantzig uses residual inner-product invariance. The closest prior CausalSmith triple-frontier proposal was downgraded because its frontier object risked being a restatement of estimator equality rather than a primitive covariance/projection condition.
open_problems.why_nontrivial: The natural attempt to define K as the vector of pairwise estimator disagreements fails because the prior reviewer required an explicit null projection-gap condition and the cited papers define the procedures by different invariance equations rather than a shared population loss.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: An if-and-only-if theorem giving rank and kernel conditions for a hand-derived covariance pencil K(Sigma_XYA), built from the named population estimating equations, that is zero exactly when the three procedure outputs agree.

open_problems.open_problem: Determine the sharp covariance conditions under which the gamma-infinity anchor-regression coefficient and the Causal-Dantzig coefficient set are identical, including invalid-anchor and nonunique-solution regimes.

source_refs.claim: Anchor regression allows invalid anchors and retains robustness, but the causal parameter is then no longer generally identifiable.
source_refs.author: Rothenhausler, Meinshausen, Buhlmann, Peters

source_refs.claim: Causal Dantzig can return a solution set under non-identifiability, with guarantees tied to additive-intervention residual equations.
source_refs.author: Rothenhausler, Buhlmann, Meinshausen

source_refs.claim: A GMM/environment view relates Causal-Dantzig constraints to instrument-style moment restrictions but does not give anchor-path equality criteria.
source_refs.author: Ha and Long

source_refs.excerpt: native-equivalent through first order at P exactly on the zero set
the main open work is proving the tangent equivalence and guarding rank-change boundary cases

open_problems.why_unsolved: Anchor regression and Causal Dantzig both lead to population linear equations, but the papers attach them to different intervention models and different identification claims. The prior proximal rejected angle identifies the same algebraic obstacle for selector comparisons: equality of values is not enough when the solution fiber is weakly identified and rank changes can alter first-order behavior.
open_problems.why_nontrivial: The natural attempt to identify the gamma-infinity anchor equation with IV and then with Causal Dantzig fails because anchor regression explicitly allows invalid anchors where causal parameters are not identifiable, while Causal Dantzig defines a feasibility set through additive-intervention residual equations.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A sharp linear-algebra theorem equating the anchor gamma-infinity normal equations and Causal-Dantzig feasibility equations exactly when an explicit covariance-block rank/kernel condition holds, with separate clauses for singleton and non-singleton solution sets.

open_problems.open_problem: Classify minimal 2-anchor by 2-covariate Gaussian SEM witnesses where pairwise residual moment invariance holds but ICP conditional-distribution invariance fails, and where conditional-distribution invariance holds while residual-moment criteria select a different coefficient.

source_refs.claim: ICP requires equality of the conditional distribution of Y given selected covariates across environments.
source_refs.author: Peters, Buhlmann, Meinshausen

source_refs.claim: Causal Dantzig explicitly substitutes inner-product invariance for ICP-style conditional-distribution invariance under additive interventions.
source_refs.author: Rothenhausler, Buhlmann, Meinshausen

source_refs.claim: Distributional anchor regression separates first-moment residual invariance from higher-moment or full residual-distribution invariance.
source_refs.author: Kook, Sick, Buhlmann

source_refs.excerpt: strict containment occurs exactly on the semi-algebraic region where each C_h is nonempty
the finite Gram-determinant description in dimension two are the main open pieces

open_problems.why_unsolved: The web literature names the conceptual distinction between conditional-law invariance and residual moment invariance, but it does not give a smallest-dimensional covariance catalogue for the two possible separations in the same SEM. The rejected LP/SVAR angle supplies the matching prior-proposal obstacle: individually feasible restrictions can fail to paste into one common structural object, and the finite low-dimensional determinant description was left open.
open_problems.why_nontrivial: The natural attempt to invoke Gaussianity and say moments determine distributions fails because the residual moment criteria condition on anchors or environments after projection, whereas ICP conditions on selected covariates and the prior rejected angle flags common-completion as the missing object.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: Two explicit Gaussian SEM covariance matrices, with all anchor, covariate, outcome, residual, and conditional covariance blocks computed, proving both directions of non-equivalence and a finite determinant or rank certificate of minimality.

open_problems.open_problem: Characterize the support frontier where ICP accepted sets, the gamma-infinity anchor-regression support, and Causal-Dantzig solution supports agree, strictly include one another, or disagree.

source_refs.claim: ICP returns accepted invariant predictor sets and conservative confidence statements rather than a single penalized coefficient path.
source_refs.author: Peters, Buhlmann, Meinshausen

source_refs.claim: Anchor stability can be used for distributional replicability, but weak anchor association makes the gamma-infinity endpoint and screening behavior delicate.
source_refs.author: Rothenhausler, Meinshausen, Buhlmann, Peters

source_refs.claim: Nonlinear ICP highlights that accepted-set behavior and power can depend strongly on causal structure and larger parental sets.
source_refs.author: Heinze-Deml, Peters, Meinshausen

open_problems.why_unsolved: The three literatures expose different support-like outputs: accepted invariant subsets for ICP, anchor-stability paths for anchor regression, and feasibility or selected coefficient supports for Causal Dantzig. Existing results do not identify the covariance regimes where these support notions coincide, nor the boundary where one method strictly includes or excludes another.
open_problems.why_nontrivial: The natural attempt to compare supports after solving each estimator fails because ICP acceptance is a conditional-law subset test, while anchor stability is a path property that the anchor paper treats as delicate in weak-anchor regimes.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A necessary-and-sufficient support-frontier theorem, stated as rank conditions and zero-pattern implications for covariance blocks, separating equality, strict inclusion, and disagreement among the three support notions.

open_problems.open_problem: Identify the maximal finite shift classes for which three-way agreement of ICP, anchor regression, and Causal Dantzig is preserved beyond the anchor-span perturbations covered by anchor regression.

source_refs.claim: Anchor regression gives robustness guarantees for shift interventions lying in an anchor-generated span.
source_refs.author: Rothenhausler, Meinshausen, Buhlmann, Peters

source_refs.claim: Invariant probabilistic prediction studies robustness under structural-equation shifts and notes that arbitrary shifts need not admit invariant robust probabilistic predictions.
source_refs.author: Henzi, Shen, Law, Buhlmann

source_refs.claim: DRIG generalizes anchor regression as a special case and targets more diverse noise-intervention robustness.
source_refs.author: Shen, Buhlmann, Taeb

open_problems.why_unsolved: Anchor regression gives a precise robustness theorem for a restricted anchor-shift span, while newer robustness papers broaden the perturbation class without analyzing the exact estimator-conflict frontier among ICP, anchor regression, and Causal Dantzig on the same covariance-level SEM. The missing object is not another robust-risk guarantee but the shift cone on which the three independently defined outputs remain equal.
open_problems.why_nontrivial: The natural attempt to apply anchor regression's perturbation theorem verbatim fails because that theorem protects only shifts in an anchor-generated span, while IPP and DRIG explicitly motivate broader shift classes where full invariance can fail.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A finite-shift robustness theorem specifying the maximal covariance perturbation cone under which the three estimator outputs remain equal, together with an explicit covariance counterexample outside that cone.

literature_map:
Peters, Buhlmann, and Meinshausen (2016, JRSSB) define ICP through equality of conditional distributions Y|X_S across environments and derive conservative accepted sets.
The ICP paper also notes violations, relaxations, hidden-variable issues, and cases where indirect or incomplete cause sets need not be invariant.
Rothenhausler, Buhlmann, and Meinshausen (2019, Annals of Statistics) introduce Causal Dantzig as an inner-product invariance method for linear SEMs with hidden variables under additive interventions.
Causal Dantzig is framed as computationally lighter than ICP but relies on a more restrictive intervention class and a different invariance notion.
Rothenhausler, Meinshausen, Buhlmann, and Peters (2021, JRSSB) introduce anchor regression, whose population path interpolates among partialling-out, OLS, and IV/TSLS.
Anchor regression allows invalid anchors and provides robustness under shift perturbations, but then causal parameters need not be identifiable.
Kook, Sick, and Buhlmann (2022, Statistics and Computing) generalize anchor ideas to distributional anchor regression and explicitly separate first-moment residual invariance from higher-moment or full-distribution invariance.
Ha and Long (2023) recast Causal-Dantzig and environment constraints in a GMM/instrument framework, giving algebraic proximity to IV moments but not a three-estimator equivalence frontier.
Heinze-Deml, Peters, and Meinshausen (2018, Journal of Causal Inference) show accepted-set behavior can depend strongly on model structure and power, reinforcing that support comparisons are not automatic.
Henzi, Shen, Law, and Buhlmann (2025, Biometrika) move from point prediction to probabilistic prediction and note that arbitrary shifts do not generally admit invariant robust probabilistic predictions.
Shen, Buhlmann, and Taeb (2026, JASA) generalize anchor regression through invariant gradients or noise interventions, widening the perturbation-class tension.
No located paper appears to solve the requested finite covariance-level conflict certificate or sharp rank/kernel frontier among ICP, anchor gamma-infinity, and Causal Dantzig.
prior_proposal_map:
Current qid eid_anchor_icp_dantzig_frontier/v1 has only an init pipeline JSONL and no proposal/review artifacts yet.
Most relevant mined prior qid is eid_lp_svar_nonequiv/v1: downgraded/failed LP-vs-SVAR non-equivalence proposals, rejected angles, reviews, and README all surface common-restriction collapse, primitive margin, common-law pasting, and finite witness gaps.
LP/SVAR reviewer JSONs yielded high-signal strengthening_paths: replace artificial relaxations with a named workflow, isolate the extra latent-law constraint, and upgrade generic separation to a computable iff frontier.
The closest same-shape three-procedure prior is eid_dml_orth_uniform/v1: a DML/LRO/ADML frontier whose reviewers demanded explicit covariance/projection operators, pairwise null-gap conditions, and primitive witness formulas.
A secondary adjacent source is eid_proximal_completeness/v1 angle2_rejected, which contributes the finite shifted KKT row-commutator pattern for selector equivalence under nonunique/weak identification.
Bank READMEs confirm eid_lp_svar_nonequiv was downgraded after the kernel substituted a difficult primitive non-equivalence promise, while eid_dml_orth_uniform remained a revise-level triple-estimator frontier rather than a completed theorem.
No upgrade_from parent was provided, and the study-pipeline gaps are off-topic LATE/Manski/interval-regression material rather than Track-A formalization evidence for this qid.
